summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened this Resurrection Sunday service with a powerful message titled 'Next Play,' drawing from his experience as a basketball coach. He used the analogy of moving on from a mistake in a game to illustrate the spiritual freedom available through Christ's resurrection. The core message, based on Luke 24:1-6, challenged the congregation to stop 'looking for the living among the dead'—searching for life, joy, and purpose in worldly things that ultimately lead to death. Pastor Travis emphasized that the resurrection is God's ultimate 'next play,' providing a way to move past failures, addictions, hurts, and past sins. He warned against the temptation to return to our 'Egypts'—old patterns of bondage—as the Israelites did, and exhorted listeners to walk by faith into God's promised future. The sermon highlighted the dangers of a divided loyalty between God and the world, which leads to an unsettled and unstable life, as described in James. Pastor Travis concluded with a call to move from being merely 'surrounded' by God's people to full 'surrender' to Jesus, leading to an altar call where many gave their lives to Christ for the first time.
mainPoints: The resurrection of Jesus is God's 'next play,' offering freedom from the tombs of our past failures, sins, and hurts.
We must choose to walk away from our personal 'Egypts'—old bonds and sins—and not look back, trusting God's promise of a better future.
A life of divided loyalty between God and the world leads to instability; true peace and purpose are found in full surrender to Christ.

versesMentioned.text: On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, the women took the spices they had prepared and went to the tomb. They found the stone rolled away from the tomb, but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus. While they were wondering about this, suddenly two men in clothes that gleamed like lightning stood beside them. In their fright the women bowed down with their faces to the ground, but the men said to them, 'Why do you look for the living among the dead? He is not here; he has risen!'
Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
Jesus answered, 'I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.'
Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!

Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.

All the Israelites grumbled against Moses and Aaron, and the whole assembly said to them, 'If only we had died in Egypt! Or in this wilderness! Why is the LORD bringing us to this land only to let us fall by the sword? Our wives and children will be taken as plunder. Wouldn’t it be better for us to go back to Egypt?' And they said to each other, 'We should choose a leader and go back to Egypt.'
Forget the former things; do not dwell on the past. See, I am doing a new thing! Now it springs up; do you not perceive it? I am making a way in the wilderness and streams in the wasteland.
But Lot’s wife looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.
No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.

But when you ask, you must believe and not doubt, because the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea, blown and tossed by the wind. That person should not expect to receive anything from the Lord. Such a person is double-minded and unstable in all they do.

But if serving the LORD seems undesirable to you, then choose for yourselves this day whom you will serve, whether the gods your ancestors served beyond the Euphrates, or the gods of the Amorites, in whose land you are living. But as for me and my household, we will serve the LORD.

Monday.scripture: Luke 24:5-6 (NIV) - 'Why do you look for the living among the dead? He is not here; he has risen!'
Monday.focus: Recognizing where you search for life.
Monday.action: Identify one 'dead' thing (a habit, thought pattern, or pursuit) you look to for fulfillment instead of Christ.
Monday.prayer: Lord, open my eyes to see where I am looking for life in the wrong places. Help me to seek You first.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:17 (NIV) - 'Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!'
Tuesday.focus: Embracing your new identity in Christ.
Tuesday.action: Write down one thing from your 'old' life that Christ has freed you from. Thank Him for making you new.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, thank you for making me a new creation. Help me to live today in the freedom and identity you have given me.

Wednesday.scripture: Philippians 3:13-14 (NIV) - 'Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead...'
Wednesday.action: When a past failure or regret comes to mind today, consciously say 'next play' and choose one positive, forward-looking thought or action.
Wednesday.prayer: God, give me the strength to forget what is behind me. Help me to strain toward the future you have for me.

Thursday.scripture: Isaiah 43:18-19 (NIV) - 'Forget the former things; do not dwell on the past. See, I am doing a new thing!'
Thursday.action: Look for one sign of God's 'new thing' in your life this week—a new opportunity, a changed perspective, or a moment of peace.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I choose to forget the past. Help me to perceive and trust the new thing you are doing in my life right now.

Friday.scripture: James 1:7-8 (NIV) - 'That person should not expect to receive anything from the Lord. Such a person is double-minded and unstable in all they do.'
Friday.action: In a moment of quiet, ask the Holy Spirit to reveal any area where your loyalty is divided between God and the world. Write it down.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, search my heart. Show me any area of divided loyalty, and give me the courage to choose wholehearted devotion to You.

Saturday.scripture: Joshua 24:15 (NIV) - '...choose for yourselves this day whom you will serve... But as for me and my household, we will serve the LORD.'
Saturday.action: Make a practical choice today that reflects your decision to serve God. This could be choosing worship over worry, or kindness over criticism.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, today I choose You. As for me, I will serve the Lord. Help my actions to reflect this choice in every part of my day.
